description: Prioritized validation matrix for the browser 2D-FDTD simulator.

smoke.purpose: Fast guard against load failures, non-finite fields, and broken presets.

physics.purpose: Numerical/physical validation intended for manual or scripted convergence runs.

convergence.purpose: Grid refinement for results used in teaching notes or publications.

checks: loads without console errors
step counter advances
max field remains finite
energy remains finite
cases.rationale: Baseline homogeneous Yee propagation. If this fails, no higher-level validation is meaningful.

checks: finite fields
line-monitor R is bounded
raw line-monitor T remains finite and documented as a port observable
compare R against normal-incidence Fresnel reference
cases.rationale: First quantitative check for interface monitors and material impedance.

checks: angle sweep runs
TM runtime reflectance remains small at the analytical Brewster angle
analytical sweep minimum is near arctan(n2/n1)
cases.rationale: Validates oblique incidence, polarization handling, and sweep diagnostics.

checks: pulse leaves the domain
late-time residual energy decreases
left/right monitor reflection remains small
CPML uses graded sigma, kappa, alpha and recursive psi memory
cases.rationale: CPML quality controls every open-domain example.

checks: finite fields
guided flux is positive
field energy is concentrated near the guide
modal source launches predominantly forward with low cladding radiation
cases.rationale: Core guided-optics workflow for integrated photonics examples.

checks: finite fields after transient warmup
integrated Ez^2 is sampled in the two output-guide cores
upper/lower output field-energy split is close to 50/50
cases.rationale: Checks that the MMI scene behaves as a balanced 1x2 splitter rather than only drawing a multimode waveguide.

checks: mode-profile source launches into the input guide
two separated interferometer arms are present
the phase-shifter arm section is represented by a distinct high-index material
field energy reaches the splitter, both arms, combiner, and output guide
cases.rationale: Checks that the Mach-Zehnder example is a real split-arm guided layout with finite energy transfer through the interferometer path.

checks: analysis trace fills
energy envelope decays
estimated ringdown Q is finite and positive
cases.rationale: Checks spectral/ringdown post-processing used by resonator teaching scenes.

checks: point-dipole source is embedded in a nested dielectric 2D cavity
analysis trace accumulates enough samples
2D Q/Aeff cavity metric is finite and bounded
cases.rationale: Checks the 2D cavity source as a bounded teaching metric: finite ringdown Q divided by effective field area in lambda0 squared.

checks: ADE material loads
finite fields after several steps
active media diagnostics report ADE
cases.rationale: Fast guard for dispersive material state arrays and diagnostics.

checks: homogeneous dielectric fills the active domain
single line source is present
finite field energy is injected in the source neighborhood
cases.rationale: Checks the dielectric plane-wave example as a homogeneous-medium Maxwell route rather than only a preset label.

checks: Gaussian temporal source type is selected
single line source is present
fields remain finite after pulse launch
cases.rationale: Checks that the Gaussian-pulse scene exercises the broadband source path, not the continuous-wave default.

checks: two point sources are present
sources are vertically separated by about one wavelength or more
source-neighborhood energy remains finite
cases.rationale: Checks the interference example as a genuine two-source coherent geometry.

checks: two point sources are present
source frequencies are deliberately detuned
source-neighborhood energy remains finite
cases.rationale: Checks the beating example as two coherent emitters with distinct temporal frequencies.

checks: PEC screen is present
finite circular aperture remains open around the optical axis
incident line source is present
cases.rationale: Checks the circular-aperture diffraction scene as a real PEC screen with an open aperture.

checks: Hz-polarized solver route is selected
angle sweep metadata is configured
dielectric half-space is present
cases.rationale: Checks the TE/TM comparison scene as an oblique-interface sweep with explicit polarization metadata.

checks: Poynting-vector view is selected
field quiver overlay is enabled
oblique line source is present
cases.rationale: Checks that the Poynting example actually selects a flux-oriented visualization and oblique propagation.

checks: evanescent line source route is selected
configured k_parallel/k0 is greater than unity
fields remain finite near the evanescent source
cases.rationale: Checks the evanescent-wave scene as an explicit supercritical tangential-wavenumber source.

checks: Gaussian-profile beam source is obliquely incident
dielectric half-space is present
field energy remains finite after launch
cases.rationale: Checks the oblique-refraction scene as a finite-width beam incident on a dielectric interface.

checks: Hz-polarized solver route is selected
Brewster-angle source and angle sweep are configured
dielectric half-space is present
cases.rationale: Checks the Brewster TE/TM scene as an angle-sweep interface example with the source near the analytical Brewster angle.

checks: dielectric half-space is present
loss coefficient is nonzero in the transmitted medium
incident line source is present
cases.rationale: Checks the lossy-interface scene as an absorptive dielectric interface, not merely a refractive-index step.

checks: anisotropic dielectric half-space is present
epsilon tensor components differ in the material region
oblique Gaussian-profile source is present
cases.rationale: Checks the anisotropic-interface scene as a tensor-material route with oblique excitation.

checks: finite transmitted field behind the aperture
downstream diffraction profile is approximately symmetric about the optical axis
central diffraction lobe is stronger than the mean profile level
cases.rationale: Turns the single-slit scene from a visual aperture into a reproducible diffraction-profile check.

checks: finite transmitted field behind the two apertures
downstream profile remains approximately mirror-symmetric
profile contains multiple resolved maxima rather than one broad leak
cases.rationale: Checks that the double-slit example behaves as an interference demonstration, not merely as two holes in a PEC wall.

checks: Drude material flag and model are active
dispersive cell mask is populated
ADE polarization/current state responds to the incident field
cases.rationale: Adds a dynamic ADE-state validation beyond the fast active-media smoke label.

checks: conductive half-space cells are present
J = sigma E update path is active
field energy enters the conductor and the pulse is damped at late time
cases.rationale: Checks the finite-conductivity scene as an explicit conductive damping route, without claiming a calibrated skin-depth fit.

checks: Lorentz ADE cells are present
effective permittivity is elevated near the configured resonance
fields remain finite while the Lorentz slab is active
cases.rationale: Checks that the Lorentz scene uses the resonant ADE branch and produces a finite dispersive-medium interaction.

checks: Debye ADE cells are present
effective permittivity exceeds the high-frequency baseline
fields remain finite while the Debye slab is active
cases.rationale: Checks that the Debye relaxation example is wired to the Debye ADE branch with a physically distinct epsilon response.

checks: plasma ADE cells are present
effective permittivity is below zero at the diagnostic frequency
fields remain finite while the plasma slab is active
cases.rationale: Checks the plasma-cutoff scene as a negative-effective-epsilon ADE medium at the teaching frequency.

checks: Drude ENZ slab cells are present
effective permittivity is close to zero at the diagnostic frequency
fields remain finite while the thin ENZ slab is active
cases.rationale: Guards the ENZ teaching example by checking the actual effective epsilon tuning rather than only the scene label.

checks: anisotropic eps_x/eps_y block cells are present
tensor diagnostics inspect the anisotropic cells
localized source deposits finite energy inside the block
cases.rationale: Checks the anisotropic-medium scene as a real tensor-epsilon block with finite field interaction.

checks: passive anisotropic Drude tensor block cells are present
effective tensor eigenvalues are indefinite near the source frequency
point-dipole source produces finite energy without nondispersive negative-epsilon blow-up
cases.rationale: Checks the scene as a passive 2D anisotropic Drude block whose effective tensor is indefinite at the source frequency.

checks: Hz six-field bianisotropy path is active
kappa is passivity-limited and nonzero
cross-field energy remains finite inside the bianisotropic block
cases.rationale: Checks the passivity-limited six-field bianisotropic coupling route with finite cross-field energy.

checks: Hz six-field bianisotropy path is active
anisotropic eps/mu block has nonzero kappa
cross-field energy and passivity diagnostics remain finite
cases.rationale: Checks that the bianisotropic block exercises the intended six-field coupling rather than a scalar fallback.

checks: Hz solver is active for the gyrotropic tensor example
antisymmetric epsilon tensor cells are present
field energy overlaps the gyrotropic block
cases.rationale: Checks the local Hz gyrotropic tensor route with nonzero antisymmetric coupling and finite material-region field overlap.

checks: the stack contains many alternating quarter-wave material segments
line-monitor reflectance is measurably above a weak-interface baseline
transmitted-side field energy is not dominant over the multilayer stack
cases.rationale: Covers the Bragg-stack teaching scene as a periodic dielectric mirror rather than only validating the earlier Bragg-mirror alias.

checks: the scene builds a two-dimensional high-index rod lattice
the Bloch-k sweep and compact plane-wave estimate are active
the band estimate reports finite bands and a nonzero gap observable
cases.rationale: Checks that the photonic-crystal example is an actual periodic lattice with an executable Bloch/PWE teaching observable.

checks: the central rod is removed from an otherwise periodic lattice
a point dipole excites the defect region
the compact Bloch-k/PWE estimate remains available for the surrounding lattice
cases.rationale: Checks the point-defect PhC as a missing-rod lattice with local field overlap and a compact surrounding-lattice Bloch/PWE reference.

checks: the central lattice row is removed to create a line defect
adjacent PhC rows remain populated
a localized source excites the line-defect channel instead of a broad TFSF beam
line-defect channel energy is significant compared with the full domain
adjacent-row leakage remains bounded relative to line-defect energy
cases.rationale: Checks that the PhC waveguide is a real line-defect demonstration with observable channel localization, not just a correct lattice mask illuminated by a broad free-space beam.

checks: an L3-like central defect is present
nearby holes are shifted or resized by the cavity geometry
analysis and compact Bloch/PWE observables remain finite
cases.rationale: Checks the shifted/tapered L3 PhC cavity geometry, local field overlap, analysis sampling, and compact Bloch/PWE reference without claiming an optimization objective.

checks: a high-index PhC rod lattice is present
rod centroids are measurably displaced from the ideal lattice
finite field energy overlaps the disordered lattice
cases.rationale: Checks that the disorder example is not a perfect PhC clone and keeps the validation to geometry and finite interaction.

checks: the central defect is excited by two opposite-phase dipoles
the lattice remains nearly mirror symmetric
analysis produces finite leakage and compact Bloch/PWE observables
cases.rationale: Checks the symmetric PhC defect under equal-amplitude opposite-phase dipole excitation with finite local field overlap and compact leakage/PWE diagnostics.

checks: the central defect is retained
source amplitudes/phases deliberately break perfect dark-mode symmetry
the compact PhC estimate reports finite asymmetry and leakage observables
cases.rationale: Checks a weakly asymmetric PhC defect with deliberate source imbalance, geometry offset, local field overlap, and compact leakage/PWE diagnostics.

checks: an L3-like PhC defect is driven with opposite-phase symmetric dipoles
the Bloch-k sweep and compact PWE path are active
leakage and local field observables remain finite under short browser execution
cases.rationale: Checks a symmetric shifted L3 PhC cavity with equal-amplitude opposite-phase dipoles, local field overlap, and compact leakage/PWE diagnostics.

checks: a high-index bus waveguide is present
a side-coupled resonator is separated by an air gap
finite field energy reaches the resonator region during the run
cases.rationale: Guards the Fano resonator scene as an actual bus-plus-side-resonator coupling example rather than a disconnected geometry sketch.

checks: the SSH chain contains the expected number of high-index sites
the first two gaps follow the trivial d1 > d2 dimerization
the analytic SSH Bloch reference reports winding zero and a finite gap
cases.rationale: Guards the trivial SSH scene against inverted dimerization or loss of the analytic Bloch reference.

checks: the SSH chain contains the expected number of high-index sites
the first two gaps follow the topological d1 < d2 dimerization
the analytic SSH Bloch reference reports winding one and a finite gap
cases.rationale: Checks that the topological SSH scene is not accidentally identical to the trivial dimerization.

checks: the interface chain keeps the high-index SSH site pattern
the analytic SSH reference marks the topological interface
a central dipole deposits measurable energy near the interface
cases.rationale: Checks the SSH interface geometry, analytic winding/gap reference, expected interface state flag, and local field overlap without claiming a full-wave eigenmode solve.

checks: the disordered SSH interface keeps a finite high-index site chain
analysis samples and clean-limit SSH Bloch reference remain available
the analytic SSH reference still marks the interface state
cases.rationale: Checks the deterministic-disorder SSH interface as a finite site chain with clean-limit winding/gap reference and local field overlap, without claiming a full-wave eigenmode solve.

checks: the non-Hermitian SSH chain has alternating gain/loss material cells
saturable gain stabilization is active
the non-Hermitian SSH gap estimate remains finite
cases.rationale: Checks the non-Hermitian SSH route as a finite gain/loss site chain with saturable stabilization, analytic clean-limit winding, finite gap estimate, and local field overlap.

checks: the honeycomb-like dimer lattice has many high-index sublattice rods
both upper and lower halves share the same sublattice mass bias
finite field energy overlaps the lattice
cases.rationale: Checks the baseline honeycomb geometry before validating domain-wall variants.

checks: upper and lower honeycomb domains have inverted sublattice bias
the poynting/scalar view used for the teaching scene remains active
field energy is present along the straight interface channel
cases.rationale: Validates the Valley-Hall scene as an inverted-domain interface, not merely a generic honeycomb array.

checks: the left side retains a horizontal Valley-Hall domain wall
the right side turns into a vertical-domain segment
the bent channel carries finite field energy
cases.rationale: Checks that the Valley-Hall bend example changes the domain-wall geometry, preserves opposite sublattice bias across the bend, and stores finite channel energy.

checks: the SSH chain is materially modulated
analysis samples are collected for the modulation route
finite field energy overlaps the modulated SSH chain
cases.rationale: Checks the modulated SSH-chain route through active material modulation, analysis sampling, site-count bounds, and finite local field overlap.

checks: the Valley-Hall inverted-domain geometry remains present
a strong PEC defect occupies the channel region
finite field energy reaches the defect/channel region
cases.rationale: Checks that the topology-with-defect scene contains both the domain wall and the intended strong scatterer.

checks: coating and substrate material segments are present
line-monitor reflectance remains bounded for the coated interface
field energy overlaps the multilayer region during the run
cases.rationale: Provides an executable sanity check that the quarter-wave coating scene is a multilayer antireflection example, not just a bare interface.

checks: many alternating material segments are present
line-monitor reflectance is measurably above a weak-interface baseline
transmitted-side field energy is not dominant over the stack energy
cases.rationale: Checks that the Bragg mirror behaves as a reflective periodic stack at the teaching level.

checks: high-index incident medium terminates at an air interface
interface band stores measurable evanescent/near-field energy
far-side air energy remains bounded relative to interface energy
cases.rationale: Turns total internal reflection into a measurable evanescent-field scene instead of relying only on the field snapshot.

checks: second high-index medium is present beyond the air gap
central air gap is narrow enough to support evanescent overlap
interface/gap band stores measurable evanescent energy
far-side high-index region receives tunneled field energy above the isolated-TIR reference
cases.rationale: Checks the finite-gap tunneling contrast expected from frustrated total internal reflection.

checks: two Bragg mirrors and central cavity material are present
cavity stores a measurable fraction of the field energy
mirror regions carry finite energy during excitation
cases.rationale: Adds a cavity-localization guard for the Fabry-Perot example without claiming a calibrated Q or linewidth.

checks: analysis trace fills for the driven cavity
central cavity line shows standing-wave contrast
Bragg mirror material cells are present
cases.rationale: Checks that the standing-wave Fabry-Perot scene produces a measurable intracavity pattern.

checks: ring and bus waveguide high-index cells are present
ring stores measurable field energy
ring/bus energy ratio is finite during modal excitation
cases.rationale: Adds a minimal ring-coupling observable before more rigorous resonance-spectrum validation.

checks: ring, bus, and drop waveguide cells are present
ring stores measurable field energy
drop bus receives finite coupled energy
cases.rationale: Checks that the add-drop ring example includes bus and drop ports, stores field energy in the ring, and transfers finite energy to the drop bus.

checks: wide slab core is present
two mode-profile sources are configured
the second source has a higher transverse mode order
cases.rationale: Checks the multimode slab as a deliberately wider guide with two modal excitations, not as a copy of the single-mode guide.

checks: single mode-profile source is present
guide core has nonzero loss
finite guided-band energy is present
cases.rationale: Checks that the lossy-guide scene exercises an absorptive guided-core route.

checks: mode-profile source launches into the horizontal guide
high-index guide centroid shifts into the bend
off-axis guide section is present
cases.rationale: Checks the bend scene as an L-shaped high-index route rather than a straight guide.

checks: mode-profile source is present
guide width changes across the device
adjacent-column width changes remain gradual
cases.rationale: Checks the taper as a gradual width-transition example.

checks: mode-profile source is present
narrow and wide guide sections are present
the width transition is abrupt
cases.rationale: Checks the width-step scene as an abrupt discontinuity, distinct from the taper.

checks: two separated high-index guides are present
mode-profile source excites one guide
many columns contain two guide segments
cases.rationale: Checks the directional coupler as two parallel guides with finite separation.

checks: straight guide is present
small off-axis dielectric perturbation is present
mode-profile source is present
cases.rationale: Checks the guide-scatterer scene as a finite perturbation coupled to a guided mode.

checks: Hz formulation is selected for the transverse cross-section field model
PEC strip and ground plane are present
dielectric substrate and finite excitation are present
cases.rationale: Checks that the microstrip example is a real 2D Hz PEC/substrate cross-section with strip-ground separation and finite substrate field energy.

checks: straight bus guide is present
side stub high-index branch is present
stub has no PEC short at its end
cases.rationale: Checks the stub resonator as a side-coupled dielectric branch rather than a straight guide.

checks: bus guide is present
racetrack annulus cells are present
analysis trace fills during modal excitation
cases.rationale: Checks the racetrack example as an elongated side-coupled resonator with analysis enabled.

checks: central high-index disk is present
single point-dipole source is inside the cavity
source-overlap energy remains finite
cases.rationale: Checks the dielectric cavity as a localized high-index disk driven by an internal dipole.

checks: central PEC frame is present
single Gaussian point source is inside the frame
source-overlap energy remains finite
cases.rationale: Checks the PEC cavity as a finite conducting frame excited by a localized source.

checks: bus guide is present
quarter-wave dielectric stub is present
stub terminates in a PEC short
cases.rationale: Checks the quarter-wave cavity as a side stub with a shorted end, distinct from the open dielectric stub.

checks: slab guide is present
single Gaussian dipole source is coupled to the guide
analysis reports a finite bounded guided-flux ratio
cases.rationale: Checks the 2D dipole-to-guide scene as a finite guided-flux ratio with a long dielectric guide and live analysis samples.

checks: central high-index disk is present
two separated off-center Gaussian dipole sources are present
analysis reports a finite bounded spectral split
cases.rationale: Checks the disk-cavity spectrum scene as two separated dipole drives exciting a finite two-peak spectral split in the 2D analysis trace.

checks: PEC cylinder cells are present
the incident field reaches the scatterer region
central downstream energy is bounded relative to the upstream incident band
cases.rationale: Guards the PEC-cylinder scene as a real obstacle/scatterer instead of an empty plane-wave case.

checks: lossless high-index dielectric cylinder cells are present
field energy overlaps the cylinder
off-axis near-field scattering exceeds the downstream back region
cases.rationale: Checks the lossless 2D dielectric-cylinder scene as a finite near-field scattering interaction under plane-wave illumination.

checks: high-index cylinder cells are present
the broadband pulse excites energy in the scatterer region
near-field scattering outside the forward channel is finite
cases.rationale: Provides a first executable guard for the Mie-like high-index cylinder without claiming a calibrated 2D Mie spectrum.

checks: PEC cylinder cells are present
analysis samples accumulate
the 2D NTFF scattering-width estimate is finite and directionally nontrivial
cases.rationale: Checks the PEC-cylinder NTFF scene as a finite 2D scattering-width workflow with incident-background subtraction.

checks: lossy high-index cylinder cells are present
field energy reaches the lossy region
downstream channel energy is attenuated by loss and scattering
cases.rationale: Checks the absorbing dielectric cylinder as a lossy 2D scatterer with finite in-object field energy and bounded downstream channel energy.

checks: two high-index scatterers are present as a dimer
the gap carries finite near-field energy
off-axis scattering remains finite
cases.rationale: Guards the dielectric dimer as a coupled near-field example rather than two inert visual disks.

checks: Hz polarization is active for the forward/backward contrast example
analysis samples accumulate
2D NTFF forward/backward scattering contrast is finite and strongly asymmetric
cases.rationale: Checks the 2D high-index-cylinder NTFF workflow for a strong forward/backward scattering contrast under Hz illumination.

checks: deterministic scatterer cluster is present
field energy spreads outside the central incident channel
transmitted channel remains finite
cases.rationale: Adds an executable multiple-scattering spread check without claiming a diffusion coefficient.

checks: many weak disorder inclusions are present
lateral speckle-like energy is finite
forward channel is not empty
cases.rationale: Guards the weak-localization scene as a disorder/spread example, not a calibrated coherent-backscattering validation.

checks: dense deterministic high-contrast disorder is present
analysis samples accumulate
energy is laterally scattered and right-boundary transport is bounded
cases.rationale: Checks the dense-disorder scene as a bounded-transport and lateral-scattering teaching example, without claiming a calibrated Anderson localization length.

checks: extended weak random medium is present
analysis samples accumulate
lateral spread and finite forward transport are measurable
cases.rationale: Checks the diffusive-random-medium example for finite spread and transport without claiming a fitted mean free path.

checks: scene uses Hz polarization and Drude metal
surface band around the metal-dielectric interface carries measurable energy
surface energy exceeds far-air and bulk-metal comparison bands
cases.rationale: Checks that the SPP interface scene actually excites an interface-localized field component.

checks: scene uses Hz polarization and Drude metal
grating teeth are present above the base metal interface
surface band around the grating/interface carries measurable energy
cases.rationale: Checks that the shallow Drude grating is present and launches measurable interface-localized Hz energy under oblique illumination.

checks: a Drude disk is present at the expected localized-plasmon position
field energy overlaps the dispersive nanodisk
near-field energy around the disk remains finite
cases.rationale: Checks the localized plasmon scene as an actual Drude nanodisk with finite field coupling.

checks: two Drude disks are present
the narrow gap between disks is geometrically open
the gap stores finite field energy during the run
cases.rationale: Guards the plasmonic dimer as a two-particle hot-gap example instead of a single merged object.

checks: the dielectric bar array is present
bar heights span a clear phase-gradient range
field energy overlaps the metasurface column
cases.rationale: Checks the metasurface phase-gradient teaching scene as a real graded bar array.

checks: a graded lossy sheet is backed by a PEC layer
line diagnostics collect finite samples
line-monitor balance estimates high absorption and low reflection
cases.rationale: Checks the backed graded-loss absorber as a near-unity line-monitor absorption example with a PEC transmission block and low reflected carrier power.

checks: analysis metrics are populated
effective epsilon and mu form a double-negative band at the diagnostic frequency
normal-incidence field and phase-front energy are finite inside the slab
cases.rationale: Checks the slab as a passive double-negative Drude ADE material with n_eff < 0 and measurable normal-incidence field/phase energy.

checks: analysis metrics are populated
effective epsilon and mu form a double-negative band at the diagnostic frequency
point-source image-plane transfer is finite and narrower than the object-plane width
cases.rationale: Checks a finite double-negative slab with point-source excitation, nonzero image-plane transfer, and a narrower image-plane width under the current 2D ADE model.

checks: the Hz solver and Drude tensor route are active
the annular hyperbolic medium contains tensor-like dispersive material
inner-to-outer ring sampling produces finite angular-harmonic transfer samples
cases.rationale: Checks the 2D hyperbolic annular-transfer scene through Hz Drude-tensor routing, material energy overlap, and finite angular-harmonic transfer.

checks: a Drude ENZ slab is present near the dipole
the diagnostic effective permittivity is near-zero in magnitude
field energy overlaps the ENZ material region
cases.rationale: Checks the ENZ emitter scene as a real near-dipole dispersive slab with finite overlap.

checks: Kerr nonlinear material is enabled
the nonlinear slab contains many active cells
field energy overlaps the slab after the wave reaches it
cases.rationale: Guards the Kerr slab as an active nonlinear-material scene rather than a passive dielectric slab.

checks: chi2 harmonic material is enabled
analysis samples accumulate
the second-harmonic response is finite and dominates the third-harmonic readout
cases.rationale: Checks that the chi2 slab runs through the harmonic route with substantial H2 output and H2-dominant harmonic content.

checks: chi3 harmonic material is enabled
analysis samples accumulate
the third-harmonic readout is finite
cases.rationale: Checks that the chi3 harmonic slab route is active and reports a finite H3 readout without claiming calibrated THG efficiency.

checks: Kerr nonlinear guide/slab is enabled
analysis samples accumulate
sideband response and active-section field overlap are finite
cases.rationale: Checks that the Kerr pulse scene routes through the nonlinear material with measurable active-section overlap and finite sideband response.

checks: Kerr nonlinear cavity material is present
mode-launched guide energy reaches the cavity/active section
analysis samples accumulate for the Kerr ring-cavity route
cases.rationale: Checks the Kerr ring-cavity scene as a mode-launched nonlinear resonator geometry with finite guide and active-section field overlap.

checks: phase-change material is enabled
the VO2-like slab contains many phase-state cells
the phase-state variable and switched-cell count respond during the run
cases.rationale: Checks the VO2 switching slab as a finite-threshold phase-state scene without claiming a calibrated transition curve.

checks: a compact phase-change memory cell is embedded in a guide
analysis samples accumulate
the compact cell reaches a persistent switched phase state
cases.rationale: Guards the PCM memory-cell scene as a phase-change guide device with finite field overlap.

checks: phase-state saturable absorber material is enabled
loss contrast exists between off/on state settings
the bleachable phase-state variable responds during the run
cases.rationale: Checks the saturable absorber as an active lossy phase-state section with measurable bleaching response.

checks: Kerr and phase-change routes are both enabled
the active control section contains nonlinear/phase-change cells
mode-launched energy reaches the active region and output arms
cases.rationale: Checks the all-optical active-section scene as a mode-launched Kerr/phase-state workflow with finite active-region and output-arm field overlap.

checks: phase-state lossy limiter material is enabled
the limiter has a strong high-intensity loss state
field energy overlaps the limiter section
cases.rationale: Checks the nonlinear limiter as a finite active-loss section with phase-state response and limiter-region field overlap.

checks: exported scene snapshot is serializable
snapshot includes grid dimensions
snapshot includes drawn material cells
cases.rationale: Ensures examples can be reproduced across sessions.

checks: evanescent source stores explicit k_parallel/k0 > 1
evanescent injection applies tangential phase and normal exponential decay
in-plane electric dipole preset forces the Hz solver and injects localized Jx/Jy
progressive dipole array phase step is derived from the target steering angle
compare source patterns against analytic phase and envelope references
cases.rationale: Tracks Atlas 11, 24, and 32 after replacing ambiguous source parameters with explicit physics metadata.

checks: single Ez point-dipole source is present
field energy remains finite around the localized source
cases.rationale: Checks the Jz dipole scene as a real localized Ez-source route.

checks: Hz solver is selected
in-plane electric-current dipole source is present
field energy remains finite around the localized source
cases.rationale: Checks the in-plane dipole scene as a distinct Hz-polarized source route.

checks: Hz solver is selected
localized magnetic-current Mz source is present
homogeneous domain keeps the point-source radiation approximately symmetric
field energy remains finite around the source
cases.rationale: Checks the Mz dipole as a localized magnetic-current source in the Hz formulation.

checks: single point dipole is present above a dielectric substrate
the substrate occupies a finite material region
source-neighborhood field energy remains finite
cases.rationale: Checks the dipole-substrate example as a localized source coupled to a finite dielectric half-space.

checks: single point dipole is present
a finite PEC mirror is present near the source
source-neighborhood field energy remains finite
cases.rationale: Checks the dipole-near-PEC scene as a real source plus mirror geometry.

checks: Huygens source route is selected
field energy is biased toward the forward side
source-neighborhood field energy remains finite
cases.rationale: Checks the Huygens radiator as a directional source route rather than an ordinary point source.

checks: circular quadrature dipole source route is selected
localized source energy remains finite
cases.rationale: Checks the circular dipole scene as the quadrature localized-source route.

checks: Janus source route is selected
a high-index guide is present near the source
directional guide-side energy is finite
cases.rationale: Checks the Janus dipole scene as a directional source coupled to a high-index guide.

checks: eight equal-phase point dipoles are present
the array is vertical with uniform half-wavelength spacing
phase-step remains near zero
cases.rationale: Checks the equal-phase dipole array geometry and phase contract.

checks: eight point dipoles are present
the array spans multiple wavelengths in y
a nonzero progressive phase step is applied consistently
cases.rationale: Checks the phased dipole array as a progressive-phase steering example.

checks: a point source is placed behind a PEC screen
a finite slot remains open in the screen
source-neighborhood field energy remains finite
cases.rationale: Checks the aperture radiator as a source plus slotted PEC-screen geometry.

checks: NTFF analysis samples accumulate
finite normalized far-field angular samples are generated
point-source NTFF pattern is nontrivial and bounded across angles
localized source energy remains finite
cases.rationale: Checks the NTFF scene as a finite near-to-far angular-pattern workflow, not a calibrated antenna pattern.

checks: line monitors report finite separated incident, reflected, and transmitted powers
oblique-incidence R/T does not rely on raw transverse field averaging
TE/TM angular sweep stores both numerical polarizations
Fresnel/Brewster reference curve is available for comparison
NTFF/scattering scenes expose normalized angular observables
cases.rationale: Tracks the shared monitor and sweep infrastructure used by Atlas 9, 10, 15, 16, 34, 62, 65, and related observable-heavy scenes.

checks: Kerr, phase-change, harmonic, and saturable-gain responses use physical field scale after internal renormalization
SHG and chi3 harmonic scenes report H2/H3 readouts
SPM and Kerr ring-cavity amplitude sweeps report sideband and active-section readouts
VO2, PCM, saturable, and limiter scenes report phase-state metrics
PT and EP scenes report gamma-sweep modal splitting estimates
cases.rationale: Tracks Atlas 111-120 and 131-132 after making nonlinear intensity, harmonic polarization, phase-state thresholds, and saturable gain consistent with field renormalization.

checks: finite fields
measure in-slab phase-front coherence
compare field phase and flux against an independent double-negative slab reference
beam-case power-balance residual
grid convergence
cases.rationale: Tracks future reference work for the double-negative slab beyond the current material and in-slab field checks.

checks: finite fields
accumulate input and output monitor DFT phasors
estimate truncated-order S_m0 transmitted and reflected sideband diagnostics
report measured-order power-balance residual
report modulation phase coherence to distinguish uniform temporal modulation from traveling or staggered phase modulation
de-embed reference planes
repeat with reverse and sideband incident channels
cases.rationale: Tracks calibrated Floquet scattering-matrix reference work after adding a normalized measured-order power residual and phase-coherence checks to the truncated-order S_m0 readout.

checks: uniform temporal modulation is enabled over the dielectric half-space
the modulation phase is spatially coherent after local phase compensation
the reflected Floquet sideband diagnostic is finite
cases.rationale: Checks the temporal-interface scene as a real uniform time-modulated dielectric interface with finite Floquet diagnostics.

checks: uniform temporal modulation is enabled in the finite slab
the slab contains many modulated cells
field energy overlaps the slab and Floquet sideband readout is finite
cases.rationale: Checks the temporal slab as a finite time-refraction region, not merely a passive dielectric slab.

checks: a compact time-modulated region exists
the modulation phase is uniform-in-space
analysis reports a finite reflected Floquet sideband diagnostic
cases.rationale: Turns the temporal epsilon-modulation scene into an executable Floquet sideband diagnostic while retaining calibrated-matrix reference tracking.

checks: an extended time-periodic dielectric region is present
the periodic high-index ribs remain embedded in the modulated background
the reflected Floquet sideband diagnostic is finite
cases.rationale: Checks the temporal crystal as an extended modulated lattice with finite Floquet sideband diagnostics.

checks: a mode-launched guide contains a finite modulated section
guided energy reaches the active section and output guide
the reflected sideband diagnostic is finite
cases.rationale: Checks that the modulated-guide example is a real guided active-section workflow.

checks: a guide section carries traveling epsilon modulation
the modulation phase has low spatial coherence and a finite phase velocity
guided energy overlaps the traveling-modulation section
the reflected sideband diagnostic is finite
cases.rationale: Guards the traveling-modulation scene against accidentally becoming a uniform temporal modulation.

checks: the guide uses a traveling modulated high-index section
lossy asymmetry cells are present downstream
mode-launched energy reaches the output guide
the reflected sideband diagnostic is finite
cases.rationale: Checks the scene as a traveling-modulation guide with a lossy asymmetric section, not as a calibrated isolation claim.

checks: a side-coupled high-index ring is temporally modulated
mode-launched energy reaches the guide/ring region
the reflected sideband diagnostic is finite
cases.rationale: Checks the modulated-ring scene as a genuine modulated resonator coupled to a guide.

checks: three coupled resonators are temporally modulated
their local modulation phases are staggered rather than spatially uniform
the reflected Floquet sideband diagnostic is finite
cases.rationale: Checks the Floquet-resonator example as a staggered-phase coupled-resonator chain with finite sideband diagnostics.

checks: a five-site modulated resonator chain is present
the modulation phases sample a five-step phase cycle
guided energy overlaps the resonator chain
cases.rationale: Checks the five-phase modulated resonator chain with finite sideband diagnostics.

checks: balanced gain and loss guide masks are present
the 2x2 PT modal diagnostic reports an unbroken phase
field energy overlaps the gain/loss guide pair
cases.rationale: Checks the PT coupler as a gain/loss guided system with a finite 2x2 modal diagnostic.

checks: balanced gain and loss guides are present
the 2x2 PT modal diagnostic is close to the exceptional-point condition
field energy overlaps the coupled guide region
cases.rationale: Checks the gain/loss coupler as a near-EP 2x2 modal diagnostic, without claiming full eigenmode-topology validation.

checks: the non-Hermitian lattice contains both gain and loss cells
the coupled-workflow diagnostic reports edge and gain/loss localization
field energy remains finite around the launched lattice
cases.rationale: Checks the biased non-Hermitian SSH chain as an active/lossy lattice with finite edge-localization diagnostics.

checks: the photonic-crystal cavity contains a Kerr active defect
anti-phase point dipoles excite the defect region
Bloch/leakage and active-material overlap diagnostics are finite
cases.rationale: Checks the Kerr PhC defect scene with anti-phase excitation, finite leakage diagnostic, and strong active-defect overlap.

checks: the photonic-crystal cavity contains an ENZ dispersive defect
anti-phase point dipoles excite the defect region
Bloch/leakage and dispersive active-material overlap diagnostics are finite
cases.rationale: Checks the ENZ PhC defect scene with anti-phase excitation, finite leakage diagnostic, and dispersive-defect overlap.

checks: the Valley-Hall-like guide geometry is present
the source uses the Janus dipole route
coupled-workflow material and source-overlap diagnostics are finite
cases.rationale: Checks the Janus/topological guide workflow as a real directional-source plus structured-guide scene.

checks: the source uses the Huygens route
a side-coupled high-index cavity is present
guide/cavity/source-overlap diagnostics are finite
cases.rationale: Checks the Huygens source/cavity/guide scene with finite overlap diagnostics.

checks: a Valley-Hall-like lattice contains a uniform temporally modulated segment
the coupled-workflow and Floquet diagnostics are finite
the modulation phase is spatially coherent after local compensation
cases.rationale: Checks the topology + temporal-modulation workflow as a structured guide with a real uniform modulated section.

checks: a Valley-Hall-like lattice contains a traveling-modulated segment
the modulation phase has low spatial coherence and finite phase velocity
coupled-workflow and Floquet diagnostics remain finite
cases.rationale: Checks the Valley-Hall scene as a traveling-modulated guide segment without claiming calibrated isolation.

checks: the space-time crystal contains many modulated dielectric stripes
the modulation phase is traveling rather than uniform
Floquet sideband and coupled active-material diagnostics are finite
cases.rationale: Checks the traveling-modulated stripe lattice with finite active-material and sideband diagnostics.

checks: biased non-Hermitian SSH scene reports field centroid, edge accumulation, and gain/loss localization diagnostics
Kerr and ENZ PhC defects report active-material overlap alongside Bloch-k leakage diagnostics
Janus/Valley-Hall and Huygens/cavity workflows report guide, cavity, source-overlap, and material-energy diagnostics
Valley-Hall temporal modulation and traveling stripe-lattice scenes expose coupled workflow diagnostics together with Floquet sideband readouts
metrics remain finite during short browser execution

acceptance.status: first-coupled-workflow-diagnostic-pass; calibrated eigenmode, non-Bloch, and device-level validation pending

cases.rationale: Tracks Atlas 133-140 after adding scene-level diagnostics for composite workflows rather than relying only on reused component diagnostics.

checks: finite fields
measure inner and outer ring field transfer
measure normalized angular-harmonic MTF
estimate MTF50 angular order
compare against controlled subwavelength source pattern
grid convergence
cases.rationale: Tracks future reference work for the 2D hyperbolic annular-transfer scene after adding angular-harmonic transfer metrics from FDTD field samples.

checks: six-field path finite
cross-polarized energy finite
output-line cross-polarized conversion finite
output-line primary/cross correlation and kappa-signed convention finite
passivity margin positive
compare against a calibrated optical-activity or tensor-parameter reference
grid convergence
cases.rationale: Tracks out-of-scene reference work for six-field bianisotropy after adding direct conversion and passivity-margin observables.
